```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
set.seed(sample.int(.Machine$integer.max, 1))
## Parametros
lambda <- sample(c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, 10), 1)

## Resposta correta e alternativas incorretas
correct <- round(1 / lambda, 4)
w1 <- round(lambda, 4)
w2 <- round(1 / lambda^2, 4)
w3 <- round(sqrt(1 / lambda), 4)
w4 <- round(1 / (lambda + 1), 4)

all_vals <- c(correct, w1, w2, w3, w4)
# Garante cinco alternativas numericamente distintas após a parametrização.
integer_choices <- all(abs(all_vals - round(all_vals)) < sqrt(.Machine$double.eps))
choice_step <- if (integer_choices) 1 else max(1e-4, max(abs(all_vals), na.rm = TRUE) * 0.01)
while (anyDuplicated(all_vals)) {
  duplicate_position <- which(duplicated(all_vals))[1]
  all_vals[duplicate_position] <- max(all_vals, na.rm = TRUE) + choice_step
}
choices <- sample(all_vals)
sol_pos <- which(choices == correct)
sol_str <- paste(as.integer(seq_along(choices) == sol_pos), collapse = "")
```

Question
========
Seja $X \sim \text{Poisson}(\lambda)$, com funcao de probabilidade:
$$P(X = x; \lambda) = \frac{e^{-\lambda}\lambda^x}{x!}, \quad x = 0, 1, 2, \ldots$$

A **informacao de Fisher** sobre o parametro $\lambda$ e:
$$I(\lambda) = -E\!\left[\frac{\partial^2}{\partial \lambda^2}\log f(X;\lambda)\right] = \frac{1}{\lambda}.$$

Para $\lambda = `r lambda`$, qual e o valor de $I(\lambda)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r choices[1]`
* `r choices[2]`
* `r choices[3]`
* `r choices[4]`
* `r choices[5]`

Solution
========
Para $X \sim \text{Poisson}(\lambda)$:
$$\log f(x; \lambda) = x\log\lambda - \lambda - \log(x!).$$
$$\frac{\partial}{\partial\lambda}\log f = \frac{x}{\lambda} - 1, \qquad \frac{\partial^2}{\partial\lambda^2}\log f = -\frac{x}{\lambda^2}.$$
$$I(\lambda) = -E\!\left[-\frac{X}{\lambda^2}\right] = \frac{E(X)}{\lambda^2} = \frac{\lambda}{\lambda^2} = \frac{1}{\lambda}.$$

Para $\lambda = `r lambda`$:
$$I(`r lambda`) = \frac{1}{`r lambda`} = `r correct`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap4S2Q2_Fisher_Poisson
extype: schoice
exsolution: `r sol_str`
exshuffle: FALSE
